# Lotus failures — vox Telegram bridge gate 2.5

Three failures recorded after devil critique 2026-05-01. Per lotus-anti-rationalization principle.

## F1 — Assumed token-stream schema without verifying
Pre-pdm direction designed debounce engine, rate-limit batching, flush-on-tool_use boundary — all predicated on token-level streaming. Did not run `jq .type` on a real /tmp/launchagent-*.jsonl before deciding.

Falsifier that should have been at gate 0: "I have read a real launchagent jsonl and confirmed event granularity (token vs content-block vs turn)."

Correction: schema verified inline as content-block-atomic (atom 019de539). Debounce engine dropped from scope.

## F2 — Chose "busy reject" knowing user has ADHD
CLAUDE.md: "Simon has ADHD." Devil M3 correctly flagged "busy, try again" as the exact loop-and-retry pattern that fails ADHD users. Lotus chose serial concurrency with rejection for implementation simplicity, not user fit.

Correction: serial concurrency stands, but rejection UX changes. New: FIFO queue with explicit position reply ("queued: 1 ahead, ETA from prior session avg"). Also auto-acks every received msg with "📥 received" so Simon never wonders if it landed.

## F3 — Named falsifier "no thread memory" then accepted scope that fires it
Gate-0 falsifier list explicitly named: "Telegram thread context unusable when each msg = new session — Simon types follow-ups, lotus has no memory of prior thread." Then chose A (one-shot) with zero compensating mechanism beyond "simaris re-prime loads identity."

Devil M4 correct: simaris re-prime ≠ thread context.

Correction options:
- (a) Inject last-N Telegram msgs from same thread into the lotus prompt (cheap, partial)
- (b) Accept the trade and tighten kill-criterion to detect when this falsifier fires
- (c) Defer to v2 — ship without and watch for the failure

Decision: (a). On thread reply (Telegram message has reply_to_message_id), pull thread history from Telegram, append as `--- prior thread ---\n<N msgs>\n--- current ---` prefix to the new task. ~10 lines. Closes the falsifier.

## F5 — Kill-criterion unmeasurable
Devil M5 correct. /tmp wipes on reboot, no count mechanism. Cannot evaluate "≥3 completed sessions in 14 days."

Correction: vox listen writes a session log line per top-level msg to `~/.local/state/vox/bridge-sessions.log` (or platform equivalent). Format: `<iso8601>\t<sid>\t<status:queued|started|completed|failed>\t<task-headline>`. Survives reboot. 14-day audit = `awk` or `jq` over this file.

## Pattern (cumulative)
F1, F2, F3 share a root: lotus made implementation-convenient choices and labeled them decisions. F1 = skipped a verification step. F2 = ignored a user-context constraint. F3 = wrote a falsifier and didn't engineer against it.

The anti-rationalization principle held. Devil caught all three. Recording per principle so the next gate-0 reads against this list.

# Context size in tokens — assistant.message.usage per turn; cache_read grows monotonically

## Source events and shape
Every `assistant` event carries `message.usage`. Per-turn, NOT cumulative across turns; but `cache_read_input_tokens` IS the running context size at that turn (the conversation history read out of cache).

Verified shape (real jsonl, /tmp/launchagent-0c63146a-...):
```
$ jq -c 'select(.type=="assistant") | .message.usage' /tmp/launchagent-0c63146a-ef97-492d-8883-f074c45faa4d.jsonl | head -1
{"input_tokens":6,"cache_creation_input_tokens":5627,"cache_read_input_tokens":21187,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":4788},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"}
```

## Per-turn vs cumulative
Per-turn `input_tokens + cache_read + cache_creation` = tokens read in to produce that turn = current context size at that point.

Verified monotonic growth across 46 assistant events in the same session:
```
$ jq -c 'select(.type=="assistant") | .message.usage | (.input_tokens + .cache_read_input_tokens + (.cache_creation_input_tokens // 0))' /tmp/launchagent-0c63146a-...jsonl
26820   # turn 1
26993   # turn 2
…
60968   # turn ~46 (last)
```

## Other sources
1. `system/init` — NO usage data.
2. `rate_limit_event` — has rate_limit_info (resetsAt, status), no token count.
3. `result` — final aggregate. `result.usage.cache_read_input_tokens` is SUM across all turns (1,267,498 in the inspected file = 28x the last per-turn value), not current context size. `result.modelUsage[model].contextWindow` reports model max (1,000,000 for opus[1m]).
   ```
   $ jq -c 'select(.type=="result") | {sum_cache_read: .usage.cache_read_input_tokens, ctx_max: .modelUsage[].contextWindow}' /tmp/launchagent-0c63146a-...jsonl
   {"sum_cache_read":1267498,"ctx_max":1000000}
   ```

## Authoritative source for "current context size"
The LATEST `assistant.message.usage` event seen so far. Specifically:
- `current_ctx_tokens = input_tokens + cache_read_input_tokens + cache_creation_input_tokens` of the most recent assistant turn.
- `model_ctx_max` (for % display) only known at end via `result.modelUsage[model].contextWindow`. Until result arrives, server can hardcode 200k/1M per model name from `system/init.model` field, OR display absolute number only.

## Banshee consumer impact
`build_timeline_event` (lib.rs:417) currently drops usage for assistant events — content is just `message.content`. To surface tokens, either:
- Extend `TimelineEvent.content` for assistant to include `usage` subset, OR
- Add `latest_usage: Option<Usage>` to `AgentState` updated by tail task on each assistant event.

Sidebar approach (latter) is simpler and matches the v2 ask (sidebar-displayed metric).

Gotchas: `wait-for`/`wait-gone`/`wait-window` exit code 3 on timeout. `loki windows` and `loki find` return empty arrays (not errors) on no match — check length before using `.[0]`. WebGL/GPU content may appear blank in screenshots. `jq` is required for JSON parsing patterns.

# Tauri app gotchas

Always launch via `.app` bundle, not bare binary. Webview reload (Cmd+R) cannot be triggered via loki. Devtools steal keyboard shortcuts — close devtools before testing keyboard interactions.
